title: Widgets in Flutter
content:
### What are Widgets?

In Flutter, everything is a widget. Widgets are the basic building blocks of the UI. They describe the visual elements and their configuration.

```dart
Widget build(BuildContext context) {
  return Container(
    padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
    child: Text('Hello Flutter'),
  );
}
```

### Widget Types

| Category | Examples | Purpose |
|----------|----------|---------|
| Layout | Row, Column, Stack, Container | Position and arrange widgets |
| Painting | Text, Image, Icon | Display visual content |
| Interactive | GestureDetector, InkWell | Handle user input |
| Styling | Padding, Align, DecoratedBox | Modify appearance |
| Navigation | Navigator, Hero | Screen transitions |

### Composition over Inheritance

Flutter uses composition - small widgets are combined to create complex UIs.

```dart
Widget build(BuildContext context) {
  return Center(
    child: Column(
      mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
      children: [
        const Icon(Icons.star, size: 48),
        const SizedBox(height: 16),
        const Text('Welcome!'),
        ElevatedButton(
          onPressed: () {},
          child: const Text('Start'),
        ),
      ],
    ),
  );
}
```

### Widget Tree

```
MyApp
  └── MaterialApp
       └── Scaffold
            ├── AppBar
            └── Body (Center)
                 └── Column
                      ├── Text
                      └── ElevatedButton
```

### Key Properties

All widgets share common properties:

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `key` | `Key?` | Controls widget identity |
| `child` | `Widget?` | Single child widget |
| `children` | `List<Widget>` | Multiple children |

> **Key Point:** Widgets are immutable descriptions of UI. When a widget's configuration changes, Flutter rebuilds the widget tree efficiently using its diffing mechanism.

quiz.question: What is the basic building block of Flutter UIs?
Are Flutter widgets mutable or immutable?
